task: I’m in a group project right now and the work is getting messy because people keep changing plans and talking over each other. Can you help me figure out a simple way to keep track of my own part and handle it if the group starts blaming someone for mistakes?

turns.agent_response:
Yes — keep it simple with **two tracks: your part and the group mess**.

## 1) Track your own part
Write down:
- what you’re responsible for
- what’s done
- what’s left
- what deadline it has

Keep your notes separate from the group’s chaos.

## 2) When the group starts blaming someone
Don’t jump into the blame fight. Say:
- “What’s the actual problem?”
- “What part is done, and what part is still open?”
- “What do we need to fix right now?”

That shifts it from arguing to working.

## 3) If they’re talking over each other
Use one of these:
- “Let’s do one person at a time.”
- “What’s the next step?”
- “Who owns this piece?”

## 4) Protect your own work
If your part is correct, keep proof:
- notes
- screenshots
- file names
- timestamps if needed

That way you can show what you actually did.

## 5) Simple rule
- **Your part:** track it clearly
- **Group blame:** don’t get dragged into it
- **Next step:** always bring it back to the work

If you want, I can turn this into a **tiny group-project script** you can use when people start arguing.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, this is useful, but I want it tighter. Give me an actual mini system I can use in class — like what to write down for my part, and one or two lines to say if they start pointing fingers. Not the whole lecture, just something I can copy fast.

turns.agent_response:
Yep — use this **tiny class system**:

## What to write down
For your part, keep 4 lines:
- **My job:** ___
- **Done:** ___
- **Left:** ___
- **Due:** ___

If you can, add:
- **Proof:** file name / screenshot / note

## If people start blaming
Say:
- “What’s the actual fix?”
- “What part is still open?”

## If they keep arguing
Say:
- “Let’s stop the blame and finish the work.”
- “Who owns this piece right now?”

## Quick rule
- **Your notes = what you did**
- **Their arguing = not your problem**
- **Your job = bring it back to the next step**

If you want, I can make this into a **one-line template** you can copy into your notebook.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s tight enough.

My notes would be:
- **My job:** ___
- **Done:** ___
- **Left:** ___
- **Due:** ___
- **Proof:** ___

If they start pointing fingers:
- “What’s the actual fix?”
- “Who has this part right now?”

If it keeps going:
- “Stop the blame. What’s next?”

Simple. I can use that.
